source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In the power grid configuration shown in the figure, it is known that \( X_{ij} = 70 \, \Omega \). The low-voltage busbar at the substation requires reverse voltage regulation (the voltage should be the rated voltage at minimum load and 105% of the rated voltage \( U_N \) at maximum load). The rated voltage of the low-voltage busbar is 10 kV, and the transformer's rated voltage is \( 110 \pm 2 \times 2.5\% / 11 \, \text{kV} \). The actual voltages referred to the high-voltage side at maximum and minimum loads are \( U_{j \text{ max }}' = 101.1 \, \text{kV} \) and \( U_{j, \text{ min }}' = 110.2 \, \text{kV} \), respectively. If \( U_i \) remains constant, the transformer tap and the capacity of the parallel capacitor to be selected are:

raw_answer_text was mapped from solution letter A to the corresponding choice text

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the configuration is 'shown in the figure'
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample is intended to depend on the image
power-grid configuration/topology is likely needed beyond the listed scalar values
asset_registry_record shows a valid image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: the task asks for two outputs jointly: transformer tap and parallel capacitor capacity
the question combines max-load and min-load conditions with reverse voltage regulation constraints
the solver must use transformer tap options together with voltage data and compensation reasoning, indicating more than one inference/calculation step
multiple-choice options are structured as paired numerical results, consistent with multi-step derivation
verifiability: answer is a concrete numerical set with exact tap ratio and capacitor capacity
choices provide discrete candidate answer sets, making final-answer checking straightforward
given values and constraints are explicit in the prompt, so a downstream verifier can check calculations against the selected pair
answer_present=true and registry_passed=true support reliable basic sample integrity

variants.rewritten_question_text: In the power grid configuration shown in the figure, it is known that \( X_{ij} = 70 \, \Omega \). The low-voltage busbar at the substation requires reverse voltage regulation: the voltage should be the rated voltage at minimum load and 105% of the rated voltage \( U_N \) at maximum load. The rated voltage of the low-voltage busbar is 10 kV, and the transformer's rated voltage is \( 110 \pm 2 \times 2.5\% / 11 \, \text{kV} \). The actual voltages referred to the high-voltage side at maximum and minimum loads are \( U_{j\,\max}' = 101.1 \, \text{kV} \) and \( U_{j,\min}' = 110.2 \, \text{kV} \), respectively. If \( U_i \) remains constant, what compensation capacitor capacity \( Q_C \), in MVAR, should be selected?

open_ended_problem_variants.title: Transformer Tap Selection
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text: In the power grid configuration shown in the figure, it is known that \( X_{ij} = 70 \, \Omega \). The low-voltage busbar at the substation requires reverse voltage regulation: the voltage should be the rated voltage at minimum load and 105% of the rated voltage \( U_N \) at maximum load. The rated voltage of the low-voltage busbar is 10 kV, and the transformer's rated voltage is \( 110 \pm 2 \times 2.5\% / 11 \, \text{kV} \). The actual voltages referred to the high-voltage side at maximum and minimum loads are \( U_{j\,\max}' = 101.1 \, \text{kV} \) and \( U_{j,\min}' = 110.2 \, \text{kV} \), respectively. If \( U_i \) remains constant, what transformer tap ratio \( K \) should be selected?

open_ended_problem_variants.title: Compensation Capacitor Capacity

The low-voltage busbar at the substation requires reverse voltage regulation: the voltage should be the rated voltage at minimum load and 105% of the rated voltage \( U_N \) at maximum load. The rated voltage of the low-voltage busbar is 10 kV, and the transformer's rated voltage is \( 110 \pm 2 \times 2.5\% / 11 \, \text{kV} \). The actual voltages referred to the high-voltage side at maximum and minimum loads are \( U_{j\,\max}' = 101.1 \, \text{kV} \) and \( U_{j,\min}' = 110.2 \, \text{kV} \), respectively. If \( U_i \) remains constant, what compensation capacitor capacity \( Q_C \), in MVAR, should be selected?
